data.title: Wholesale inflation flattens out in February in another sign of easing inflation
data.description:
By Jeffry Bartash

Producer price index settles down after recent runup

Story developing. Stay tuned for updates here.

The numbers: Wholesale prices were unchanged in February, offering more evidence that inflation pressures eased somewhat early in the new year.

The flat reading in the producer price index came in under Wall Street expectations. Economists polled by The Wall Street Journal had forecast a 0.3% increase.

The last time the producer index index showed so little inflation was last July.

The rate of wholesale inflation in the past 12 months, meanwhile, slipped to 3.2% from 3.7%.

The benign report on wholesale inflation follows a surprisingly mild increase in consumer prices last month.

Those two inflation reports are used to help compile the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation barometer known as the PCE. While prices were muted in February, the rate if inflation is still to high to spur the central bank to cut interest rates again soon.

Big picture: If President Trump's tariffs raise U.S. inflation, the first clear evidence will come in wholesale prices. So stay tuned.

A potentially bigger threat is the possibility of tariffs not only raising inflation, but causing the economy to slow. Trade wars have made businesses hesitate to hire and invest.

Market reaction: The Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA and S&P 500 SPX were set to open lower in Thursday trading.

-Jeffry Bartash

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Webuild Declares Dividend of EUR0.26 Per Savings Share
Webuild Declares Dividend of EUR0.081 Per Ordinary Share
UK ONS delays publication of Friday's trade data, citing errors
data.description:
Britain's Office for National Statistics said it would delay by two weeks the publication of trade data for January that it had planned to release on Friday, citing an error in the data.

"Due to the identification of a further error in UK trade statistics, relating to international trade in services data for 2023, the Office for National Statistics has made the decision to delay the UK Trade: January 2025 release, which was scheduled for Friday 14 March," the ONS said in a statement.

The data "will now be published on March 28, including the full suite of corrected trade in goods and services data," it said.

The ONS has been criticised by the Bank of England and investors over problems affecting its separate labour market statistics since 2023.

data.title: Deutsche Bank Expects to Moderate Pace of Dividend Hikes Beyond 2025
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

Deutsche Bank reaffirmed its target for capital returns to shareholders for the five-year period ending in 2025, but said dividends will grow more moderately beyond this year.

The German lender aims to return to shareholders at least 8 billion euros ($8.71 billion) for the 2021-2025 period to be paid until 2026, but has said it is positioned to exceed that goal. This includes plans for returns of 2.1 billion euros this year.

Since 2024, the bank has targeted returning half of its net profit to shareholders through a combination of dividends and stock buybacks.

"Deutsche Bank will continue to target a payout ratio of 50% after 2025 through share buybacks and cash dividends, with cash dividends growing more moderately compared to increases seen in recent years," it said in its annual report published Thursday.

In January, the bank said it would declare a dividend of 0.68 euros a share for 2024, amounting to 1.3 billion euros in total. It has hiked its dividends by 50% in each of the past two years and said it aims to declare a dividend of 1 euro a share for 2025, a 47% increase on year.

On top of the dividend, Deutsche Bank received supervisory authorization for a buyback of 750 million euros, which it expects to have mostly completed by the end of the third quarter, the bank said.

The bank's annual report also showed Chief Executive Christian Sewing's total compensation for last year increased to 9.8 million euros from 8.7 million euros a year before.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: Free issue of shares to the company itself
data.description:
Toivo Group Plc, company release, 13 March 2025 at 4:05 p.m.

On the basis of the authorisation granted by the Annual General Meeting held on 11 April 2024, it was decided that a total of 220,000 new company shares are to be issued free of charge to the company itself. The new shares are of the same class as the company's other shares.

The share issue is in preparation for the needs of the company management's share-based incentive system.The new shares are estimated to be entered in the trade register by 15 April 2024, after which the company will immediately apply for the shares to be admitted to public trading on the Nasdaq First North Growth Market Finland marketplace. Following the registration of the new shares, the total number of the company's shares will be 58,979,559, of which 514 964 are held by the company as its own shares, representing a total of approximately 0.9 per cent of the company’s total number of shares.

Toivo Group Plc

Further information

Markus Myllymäki

CEO

Toivo Group Plc

Tel. +358 (0)40 847 6206

markus.myllymaki@toivo.fi

Certified Adviser: Nordic Certified Advisor

About Toivo

Toivo is a Finnish real estate company that was founded in 2015. Its business consists of project development and long-term ownership of apartments. The Company’s business model is unique as Toivo’s business combines the value chain of real estate business from development and construction to ownership, management and rental of a completed property. Toivo manages the entire life cycle of properties with its own team, from raw land development to tenancy agreement negotiations and property maintenance. This way Toivo is able to generate additional value to its customers, shareholders and stakeholders.

Toivo’s strategy is to develop properties in accordance with the Toivo concept. The properties aim for a strong development margin and a stable and attractive return, and in this way enable long-term ownership and the generation of higher additional value to Toivo’s customers. Toivo has a knowledgeable and experienced team of experts with strong merits in the real estate business. The members of Toivo’s team have been involved in the development and construction of over 17,000 apartments, and they have an average of ten years of experience in the real estate business.

Toivo’s revenue in 2024 was EUR 39,8 million and its operating profit was EUR 4,5 million.

data.title: Southwest Airlines Lowers First-Quarter Fuel Cost Estimate — OPIS
data.description:
Southwest Airlines lowered its forecast for first-quarter 2025 fuel costs, according to a filing with the SEC earlier this week.

The airline now expects fuel to cost $2.35-2.45/gal on average over the first three months of the year as opposed to the previous estimate of $2.50-2.60/gal.

The revised outlook also shows a 7ct/gal premium from hedging expenses.

The SEC filing cites lower capacity estimates, "a higher-than-expected completion factor, less government travel, and a greater impact from the California wildfires than originally estimated," as well as "softness in bookings and demand as the macro environment has weakened."

Earlier this week, JetBlue also lowered its fuel cost estimates for the first quarter.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Cory Wilchek, cwilchek@opisnet.com; Editing by Michael Kelly, mkelly@opisnet.com

data.title: IonQ falls after Kerrisdale takes short position
data.description:
** Shares of quantum computing firm IonQ fall ~3% to $21.17

** Kerrisdale Capital says it has taken short position in IonQ

** Massive scaling challenges and reliance on photonic interconnects means co is nowhere close to producing commercially viable product, Kerrisdale says

** "As reality sets in on the quantum computing bubble, IONQ remains wildly inflated," investment firm says

** Reuters could not independently verify the claims in the short seller's report

** IonQ did not immediately respond to Reuters' request for comment

** Stock more than tripled in 2024

data.title: Ibovespa Steadies Amid Trade War
data.description:
The Ibovespa hovered above the flatline at the 123,800 level on Thursday, weighed down by uncertainties stemming from President Trump’s unpredictable tariff policies and potential EU retaliatory measures, which stoke fears of an escalating global trade war.

However, the Sao Paulo exchange operator B3 surged over 5% following a decisive Carf ruling that canceled a longstanding R$5.77 billion tax infraction related to the 2008 Bovespa merger, thereby alleviating a major overhang.

Mega-cap commodity producers Petrobras and Vale added around 1% each, while most other equities traded flat to negative, with heavyweights such as WEG, Banco do Brasil, Santander, Eletrobras, JBS, Compania Sanea, and Rede D'Or all losing roughly 1%.

data.title: Share issue in accordance with the terms of the share-based incentive system
data.description:
Toivo Group Plc, company release, 13 March 2025 at 4:00 p.m.

On the basis of the authorisation granted by the Annual General Meeting held on 11 April 2024, a free directed share issue was decided on for persons entitled to a share award. A maximum of 220 000 shares held by Toivo Group Plc will be issued free of charge in the share issue. data.title: ConnectM Authorizes $10 Million Stock Buyback Program
data.description:
ConnectM Technology Solutions said Thursday it has authorized a new stock buyback program of up to $10 million or 10 million shares, depending whichever threshold is met first.

The buyback program is slated to run for 18 months, the electrified equipment and mobility company said.

ConnectM said it had roughly 34.1 million shares outstanding on Feb. 28.

Shares of the company fell 5.4% in recent trading.

data.title: Lotus Technology Signs Strategic Deal With Cao Mobility
data.description:
Lotus Technology said Thursday that its Lotus Robotics unit has signed a strategic partnership agreement with Chinese ride-hailing service CaoCao Mobility

The company said the collaboration will incorporate Lotus Robotics' intelligent driving systems.

Lotus also said it is working with regional partners, including Aramco Digital in the Gulf Cooperation Council region, and that it plans to deploy its navigation capabilities in Q4.

Shares of the company were up 2% in recent trading.

data.title: ICE Canada Morning Comment: Positive Movement in Canola
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Intercontinental Exchange canola futures were higher Thursday morning on what could be bargain hunting.

Tariffs to be levied by China and the United States pushed canola down hard this week.

Despite the forthcoming levies, Canadian canola supplies continue to be tight which underpinned values.

The Canadian oilseed also gleaned support from increases in the Chicago soy complex, European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil.

Moderate declines in crude oil tempered the gains in the vegetable oils.

Yesterday, Statistics Canada projected plantings of the 2025/26 canola crop at 21.65 million acres, down 1.7 percent from last year.

The Canadian dollar was unchanged on Thursday morning, with the loonie at 69.49 U.S. cents.

Approximately 9,600 contracts were traded by 9:37 EDT and prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne were:

data.title: Cytta Corp's Cytta Labs Venture Studio Delivers 500%+ 2 Year Return on Reticulate Micro Special Purpose Vehicle
data.description:
Cytta Venture Studio Distribution Generates Gains Exceeding $10 million

LAS VEGAS, NEVADA / ACCESS Newswire / March 13, 2025 / Cytta Corp. (OTCQB:CYCA) proudly announces a landmark achievement for its Cytta Labs Venture Studio, delivering over 500% 2 year returns on its first Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV), Reticulate Micro. This milestone underscores Cytta's venture studio model, which identifies, develops, and scales cutting-edge safety and security technologies into high-growth, market-ready companies, generating exceptional investor returns.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EP48mM56RX4

The public listing of Reticulate Micro (reticulate.io) has resulted in our Direct Investor Venture Studio distribution generating gains exceeding $10 million. Through Cytta's exclusive Direct Investor Venture program, over 2.5 million shares of Reticulate Micro were directly distributed to participating investors, providing them with a unique, direct path to liquidity and exponential upside potential. Cytta Corp. continues to hold an additional $10 million in unrealized equity gains from its remaining 2.4+ million Reticulate Micro shares. This ensures further monetization and long-term shareholder value creation.

Cytta Labs Venture Studio: Building the Future of Defense & Personal Safety Tech

The Cytta Labs Venture Studio is dedicated to launching and scaling transformative companies in defense, security, and personal safety technology. By combining deep industry expertise, market-driven innovation, and investor-friendly monetization strategies, Cytta Labs has created a proven model for accelerated growth and high-yield returns.

"We don't just develop technologies - we build market-dominating companies. Through Cytta Labs, we identify and scale the most promising technological innovations in safety and security, structuring them into SPVs to maximize value for our investors and stakeholders. The 500%+ return on Reticulate Micro over a two-year period, distributed to Venture Studio investors, is just the beginning." - Gary Campbell, CEO, Cytta Corp.

With a growing portfolio that includes AI-powered security solutions, proprietary video streaming solutions, drone control command systems, fully US designed and built drones, and advanced video compression technologies, Cytta Labs is positioned at the forefront of industry disruption.

A Smarter Approach to High-Return Investing

Cytta Corp. offers a next-generation approach to high-return investment, setting itself apart from traditional venture capital and private equity. As a publicly traded company, Cytta provides early access to breakthrough innovations while enabling liquidity, rapid capital deployment, capital protection and scalable market-driven exits.

"Cytta Labs is built for speed, agility, and investor success. Our ability to launch, scale, and structure SPVs like Reticulate Micro is proof that our venture studio model delivers exceptional results." - Natalia Sokolova, President, Cytta Corp.

With this 500%+ 2-year return to investor milestone achieved, Cytta Labs Venture Studio is poised for even greater expansion, advancing its portfolio of high-growth ventures and offering investors a unique opportunity to participate in the future of safety, security, and defense technology.

Join the Future of Innovation. Invest in Cytta. For more information about Cytta Corp. and Cytta Labs Venture Studio, visit Cytta.com.

About Cytta Corp

Cytta Corp is a pioneering technology company specializing in developing and commercializing cutting-edge safety and security solutions. Our proprietary innovations redefine real-time intelligence, data security, and mission-critical communications, empowering industries such as defense, public safety, critical infrastructure, and enterprise operations.

Through Cytta Labs, our venture studio, we incubate, accelerate, and spin out breakthrough technologies, transforming them into high-growth ventures. Once market-ready, we establish Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs) to scale and consolidate these innovations, driving strategic roll-ups for maximum market impact and high value exits.

By seamlessly integrating proprietary product development, venture acceleration, and structured exit strategies, Cytta Corp is revolutionizing the future of safety, security, and intelligence on a global scale. For more information, visit www.cytta.com.

data.title: Canvas at Midlothian: A Brand New Build-to-Rent Community Now Leasing in Midlothian, TX
data.description:
MIDLOTHIAN, Texas, March 13, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Watermark Companies is proud to announce that its latest development, Canvas at Midlothian, is now accepting applications for April move-ins.

Watermark Companies, a solutions-based build-to-rent real estate developer, is known for their impressive single-family home communities - known as “Canvas Communities”. Now leasing two, three, and four bedroom homes, Canvas at Midlothian is professionally managed by Willow Bridge Property Company, a leader in the multifamily industry. Canvas at Midlothian is anticipated to welcome its first residents in April of 2025.

Canvas at Midlothian offers 201 thoughtfully designed single-family homes and townhomes in Midlothian, TX. Each home features stylish interiors and premium in-home amenities such as an attached 2-car garage, smart home technology, stainless steel appliances, and a private outdoor space. In addition to the host of niceties inside the homes, residents will also enjoy a maintenance-free lifestyle with complimentary lawn care and home repairs.

This brand-new community offers the freedom of single-family living with the conveniences of a rental community. Residents have exclusive access to a community pool, pickleball court, a fitness center with cardio and strength equipment, a spacious clubroom, and grilling stations. The pet-friendly community also offers an on-site dog park and lush green spaces throughout.

Canvas at Midlothian is situated in the rapidly growing city of Midlothian, Texas, just a 30-minute drive from both downtown Dallas and Fort Worth. Residents can enjoy access to abundant outdoor recreation, while also being minutes from shopping, dining, and entertainment options. Listed as the number two ‘Best Place to Live in Ellis County’, according to Niche.com, Midlothian offers a seamless blend of small town charm & modern luxuries.

“As build-to-rent communities continue to grow and increase in popularity, we’re excited to welcome this exceptional community to Midlothian,” said Catlin Clarke, Marketing and Communications Manager at Watermark Companies. “By offering a variety of home types and floor plans - along with a plethora of amenities - we’re able to meet the needs of a wide range of renters and elevate the way they live, work, and play.”

For more information, please visit canvasatmidlothian.com

About Watermark Companies

Watermark Companies is a solutions-based real estate developer and builder providing residents a new & improved way to live across the nation. Watermark develops impressive single-family home communities with the conveniences of a lease and the amenities of a resort, all professionally managed and maintained. Our communities distinguish themselves by revolutionizing the way residents live, work, and play through intentional design, hospitality-inspired service, and a resident-first ideology. To learn more, visit wmcompanies.com.

About Willow Bridge Property Company

Founded in 1965 by Mack Pogue as Lincoln Property Company, Willow Bridge Property Company is one of the United States’ most respected full service residential property companies, operating a successful portfolio in 75 markets. Headquartered in Dallas, Texas, Willow Bridge proudly employs over 5,200 team members, has built over 200,000 apartments and manages 220,000 units for its clients. To learn more, visit willowbridgepc.com.

Media Contact: Megan Collins, Director of Communications, Willow Bridge Property Company 214-740-3400 | corporate@willowbridgepc.com

SOURCE Willow Bridge Property Company

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/79c9adbf-5736-4666-a52f-0adfdbc13e09

Canvas at Midlothian

Canvas at Midlothian, a high-end rental home community, is now leasing 201 homes and townhomes in Midlothian, Texas.

data.title: Fiction: '33 Place Brugmann' by Alice Austen
data.description:
By Sam Sacks

The title of Alice Austen's "33 Place Brugmann" points us to the address of an apartment building in a prosperous Brussels neighborhood, a district full of Parisian-style mansions, quiet parks and elegant boutiques. The residents include the art dealer Leo Raphaël, his wife, Sophia, and their children; the architect Francois Sauvin and his daughter Charlotte, a painter; a widowed army colonel; an attorney; a notary and other well-to-do Belgians. The building and its community would stand as a model of bourgeois stability were it not for the time period: It is August 1939, and the outbreak of war is about to throw the country into chaos.

Ms. Austen's nimble debut follows this group's fortunes through the Nazi invasion and occupation of Belgium. The Raphaëls, who are Jewish, flee to Britain, where Leo and Sophia's son, Julian, joins the Royal Air Force. The others remain, negotiating dwindling food supplies and the rising threat of violence. "The occupation of Brussels was immediate," Charlotte reflects, "but the constraints — the impositions, the curfews, the ostracizing, the marking, the bans, the roundups, the deportations, the murders — these happened so gradually they might be called cunning, for just as you got used to one thing, there was another."

Neutrality is impossible under such conditions and the characters — Ms. Austen alternates among their points of view — are all ultimately forced to choose between defiance or complicity. Due to the daring work of the Sauvins, the army colonel and a Russian emigrée named Masha, who lives in the top-floor maid's quarters, 33 Place Brugmann becomes a node of the Belgian resistance, specifically in the network known as the Comet Line, which helped downed Allied airmen escape into Spain. We follow the dangerous twists of these operations, which are in all but plain view of neighbors who either collaborate with the Nazis or are terrified of crossing them.

A novel with a cast this large must either be written as a sprawling epic or, as Ms. Austen has done, as a succession of rapid scenes that form a web of crisscrossing storylines. The glancing nature of the chapters inevitably leaves certain elements unrealized. Francois, Charlotte and Leo, each an artist or art lover, all believe in a unity of "ethics and aesthetics" that should guide our actions, a tantalizing concept that doesn't materialize in tangible ways. And the recurring references to the "Tractatus Logico-Philosophicus" by Ludwig Wittgenstein — a professor of Julian's — remain as puzzling to me as that philosopher's gnomic aphorisms.

One can overlook these elisions thanks to Ms. Austen's skill at quick personality sketches and her knack for suspense. Unambiguously noble characters such as Charlotte are admirable, but just as engaging is Dirk DeBaerre from apartment 2R, a self-important student with an unpredictable moral compass whose sympathies seem to be up for grabs until the conclusion. The elegant Brussels building becomes a poignant microcosm of wartime alliances and betrayals.

The long-term damage wrought by Nazi atrocities is at the heart of Stuart Nadler's "Rooms for Vanishing." Its more concentrated focus is on the postwar lives of the Altermans, a Jewish family from Vienna whose world was ripped apart during World War II. As we learn from their memories, Fania and Arnold sent their daughter Sonja to London before they were arrested. Transported to a concentration camp, the couple were separated and their infant son, Moses, was taken from Fania's arms. All four lived through the ordeal, but each has moved on from it alone, unaware that the others are alive. The alternating sections, which alight on various moments between 1979 and 2016, take up the perspectives of each of the survivors, to explore the ghostly ways those absences have affected them.

The haunted, fable-like narratives are filled with echoes, uncanny doubles and strange voices from the void. Fania, who immigrates to Montreal and works as a hotel masseuse, treats a client who seems to share her identical backstory. Arnold, returning to Vienna for his 99th birthday, is contacted by someone claiming to be his daughter, but the woman never shows up at their meeting points. A feeling persists in each that the primal rupture of their family also broke something in the time-space continuum, so that "everyone had been survived into different futures," doomed to continue in parallel, discrete realities.

Mr. Nadler develops this melancholy notion with care and solemnity, stripping from his writing anything that might distract from its elegiac tone. There is a stark, unfurnished quality to "Rooms for Vanishing" that makes its protracted length difficult to bear . The novel has the beauty of a dirge but not the substance of a story.

Xhenet Aliu's "Everybody Says It's Everything" is fully grounded in the working-class town of Waterbury, Conn., where Drita and her twin brother, Pete, grew up with their adoptive mother, Jackie. All that the siblings know of their bloodline is that their birth mother was Albanian, a heritage that piques their curiosity only in adulthood. We first meet Drita in 1999; she's now a jaded nurse helping to care for Jackie when Pete's girlfriend, Shanda, and their son Dakota, return to town. Pete, who has a history of trouble with money and the law, has disappeared.

When Drita eventually learns where he's gone, the discovery doesn't lessen her worries. Broke and desperate for direction, Pete has fallen in with a group of Albanian-American militants who are secretly supplying arms and conscripts to Kosovo in its war against Serbian-controlled Yugoslavia. Ms. Aliu takes the novel in multiple directions at once: She digs into Pete and Drita's burgeoning fascination with Albanian nationalism while flashing back to both their youths and the events that led to their adoption.

Consistent through it all is the presence of willful, interesting women. Jackie adopted the twins despite having lost the use of her legs in a car accident. Shanda is recovering from a drug addiction. Drita is a prickly martinet who takes on too much responsibility. (Pete, a sad, sensitive screw-up, is a bore in comparison.) Their searches for belonging give the novel its rough, streetwise charm.

data.title: Aehr Test Systems Receives $4.7 Million in Follow-On Orders From  Storage Device Supplier
data.description:
Aehr Test Systems said Thursday that it had received $4.7 million in initial follow-up production orders from an unidentified storage device supplier.

The company said the orders include multiple "FOX-CP" systems as well as "WaferPak Contactors," which will be used for test and burn-in of production devices.

Aehr said it expects shipment to start in the next three to four months.

The company also said the customer has indicated plans to order additional "FOX-CP" production systems in fiscal Q4, potentially making the customer a 10% revenue contributor.
